title: Time-gated secure exam-material download for schools (question papers / OMRs)
business_reason: Schools self-select exam date + student count (no capacity blocking). Operations distributes the exam materials (question paper / OMR PDFs) to each school to PRINT, then receives the administered answer sheets back. The school must be able to securely download its materials, but ONLY within the release window — never before release_at (leak prevention) and never for revoked/superseded material. Today /api/school/materials is list-only (coarse status filter + finance gate) — there is no release-time gate and no actual secure download.

design.pure_helper: server/materials/releaseGate.ts packageReleaseGate({now, releaseAt, packageStatus, fileStatus}) -> {allowed, reason}. Honors school_access_policy.json: allowed only when packageStatus in {released, downloaded} AND release_at present AND now >= release_at; blocked for not-yet-released (scheduled/future), and for revoked/superseded package OR file. Unit-tested.
design.download_route: GET /api/school/materials/[fileId]/download — requireSchoolScope; load the file (exam_material_files) + its package (exam_material_packages); enforce own-school (package.school_id === actor.school_id, else 404, no leak); run packageReleaseGate; createSignedDownloadUrl({fileId: file_ref}); record an exam_material_download_events row; return only {download_url, expires_at, ttl_seconds} (never the storage path; never log the URL).
design.receive_side: The administered answer-sheet RETURN composes with the existing OMR import (FR-OMR-IMPORT-0010, evaluation_import_batches ingest) — a school-facing upload route is the follow-up.

param_14.question: How does a school get its exam materials, and when?
param_14.answer: A school securely downloads its question-paper / OMR PDFs via a short-lived signed URL, but ONLY within the release window: the package must be released and release_at must have passed (no early access — leak prevention), and the material must not be revoked/superseded. Access is own-school only (school_id resolved server-side, never from the browser) and every download is audited. The school prints + administers, then returns the answer sheets (OMR import).
param_14.answer_source: exam_material_ops/release_timer_policy.json (release_at_required, early_release_blocked), school_access_policy.json (school_access_allowed_after_release, own-school only, download_blocked_for_revoked_or_superseded), secure_file_policy.json; exam_material_packages.release_at/package_status, exam_material_files.file_ref/file_status
param_14.security_implications: TIME-gated (no access before release_at — prevents question-paper leaks), own-school scope resolved server-side (OWASP A01; school_id never trusted from browser; cross-school -> 404 no leak), revoked/superseded blocked, private storage + short-lived signed URL (never the raw path, never logged), every download audited. The finance gate already enforced upstream on the list route.
param_14.approval_implications: release itself (early release) requires operations_head/company_admin override + dual approval (release_timer_policy) — out of scope here; this CR is the post-release school download gate.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: school-facing answer-sheet upload (-> OMR import FR-OMR-IMPORT-0010); school_status/slot_assignment rechecks (school_access_policy) beyond release+status are a follow-up; expires_at window close (exam_materials model) if adopted.
param_14.assumptions: exam_material_files.file_ref is a file_metadata id; packages are school-scoped via school_id.
